title: Object-Oriented Architecture and Capstone Delivery · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Deliver an extensible community-resource workflow with a domain core, replaceable boundaries, migrations, and operator evidence. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a production-shaped object-oriented capstone. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate domain rules, application orchestration, infrastructure adapters, and user interface concerns with explicit dependencies.
Use domain services only for cohesive rules that do not naturally belong to one entity or value object.
Version persisted representations and migrate data without coupling domain objects directly to historical storage schemas.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Oop Layered Boundaries · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: python oop architecture capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: python oop architecture capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Oop Layered Boundaries without dropping earlier contracts?

content.text: Separate domain rules, application orchestration, infrastructure adapters, and user interface concerns with explicit dependencies. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: python oop architecture capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Oop Layered Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a production-shaped object-oriented capstone while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Layered architecture requires each layer to live in a separate deployed service. This fails the Oop Layered Boundaries contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Typing Documentation (Python 3.14.6) — Controls protocols, generics, class object types, Self, callable typing, annotation introspection, and deprecations.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Capstone Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend substitutability, invariants, typing, tests, persistence, failures, security, and maintainability with changed-case evidence.
